name: HMS Scottish (FY 245)
events: 3 Jun 1941 At 0630 A/3, HMS P 32 (Lt. D.A.B. Abdy, RN) arrived in position 36°23'N, 08°20'W in which rendezvous was to be made with the trawler (armed boarding vessel) HMS Scottish (Cdr.(Retd.) S.A. Brooks, OBE, RN) but the trawler was not there.

At 0810 A/3, HMS P 32 transmitted her signal timed 0705 A/3, which reported that HMS Scottish was not at the rendezvous and that HMS P 32 was proceeding to position 'E' keeping clear of the area in which U-boat activity had been reported.
At 0845 A/3, a fix was taken and it was discovered that HMS P 32 was 14 miles further to the east as expected.
At 1515 A/3, in position 36°04'N, 06°31'W, a merchant vessel was sighted hull down on bearing 050° at a range of 14 miles. Course of this merchant vessel was 300°.
At 1645 A/3, rendezvous was made with HMS Scottish and course was set in company to proceed to Gibraltar.
At 1725 A/3, HMS P 32 and HMS Scottish arrived in position 'E' (35°57'N, 05°58'W).
At 2230 A/3, HMS P 32 and HMS Scottish arrived at Gibraltar. (1)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
